articles.headline: Samsung Group will announce Monday plans to invest 1,000 trillion won ($647.53 billion) in South Korea over 10 years, including chip factories
articles.summary: Samsung plans a massive $647.5 billion investment in South Korea over 10 years, with a potential $194 billion allocated to new chip factories. This unprecedented capital commitment signals a major shift in global semiconductor supply chains and could affect chip pricing, availability, and competitive dynamics for businesses relying on memory and logic processors.

articles.headline: Qualcomm agreed to acquire the AI software company Modular for about $3.9 billion to make AI faster and cheaper
articles.summary: Qualcomm has acquired AI software company Modular for $3.9 billion, aiming to accelerate and reduce the cost of artificial intelligence for its customers. This strategic move suggests a shift toward more efficient, integrated AI solutions that could lower operational costs for businesses deploying AI on edge devices and mobile platforms.

articles.headline: Sharp drops in Big Tech companies pull the Nasdaq down 2.2% as global tech selloff spreads from Asia
articles.summary: A global sell-off in big technology stocks, triggered by concerns over higher interest rates and AI-related volatility, caused Micron Technology to slump 11.2% and Nvidia to fall 3.2%. Samsung Electronics also dropped 12.3% in South Korea, indicating immediate market pressure on semiconductor and AI infrastructure suppliers that could impact business planning and hardware procurement costs.
